research.summary: FYN Wellness is an emerging India-based D2C functional nutrition brand focused on cellular health. Its current hero product is Hydra, a blue spirulina cellular hydration mix made with blue spirulina, bioactive peptides, and coconut electrolytes. The brand positions hydration as a daily functional ritual for energy, recovery, skin, gut-skin support, antioxidant defence, and clean hydration without added sugar, caffeine, artificial colour, or preservatives.
research.essence: A vivid blue daily ritual that turns hydration into cellular nutrition for energy, recovery, and glow.
research.voice: Scientific, clean, aspirational, direct, founder-led, educational, ritualistic, and premium without feeling medicinal.

palette.role: Signature electric blue spirulina drink colour
Clean white packaging and negative space
Light grey ecommerce surface and UI neutral

research.aesthetic: Clean clinical-premium product photography on white, pale grey, or very light ice-blue backgrounds with smooth studio surfaces, clear glassware, and minimal props. The colour grade is cool, bright, high-clarity, and low-warmth, allowing the electric blue spirulina liquid to become the saturated focal point while packaging and surroundings stay restrained. Styling density is sparse: sachet, glass, powder bloom, water, nutrition panel, or a single hand performing the ritual; no rustic wood, Ayurveda props, dark gym scenes, or clutter. Lighting is soft diffused daylight or softbox, usually front or side lit, with gentle shadows and a hygienic luminous feel. Crops are graphic and ecommerce-led: centered pack shots, vertical sachet close-ups, transparent glass hero shots, blue liquid macro details, and clean ingredient or nutrition-panel frames. The current visual world is mostly product-only and ritual-object focused rather than model-heavy, with human presence used sparingly if at all.

photoRules.grade: Shoot on clean white or pale neutral surfaces with bright, cool, high-clarity lighting. Use sparse compositions: one sachet, one clear glass, vivid electric-blue liquid, powder bloom, water, or a hand performing the ritual. Keep props minimal, avoid rustic Ayurveda or dark gym aesthetics, and use centered graphic crops that make the saturated blue drink the focal point.
signatures: Shoot on clean white or pale neutral surfaces with bright, cool, high-clarity lighting. Use sparse compositions: one sachet, one clear glass, vivid electric-blue liquid, powder bloom, water, or a hand performing the ritual. Keep props minimal, avoid rustic Ayurveda or dark gym aesthetics, and use centered graphic crops that make the saturated blue drink the focal point.
neverDo: a floating product on a gradient void
a concentric spotlight halo behind the subject

intelligence.overview: FYN Wellness is an early-stage Indian D2C functional nutrition brand focused on cellular health. Its first product, Hydra, is a blue spirulina cellular hydration mix positioned as a clean daily ritual for hydration, energy, recovery, skin support, and antioxidant defence.
intelligence.purpose: To make nutrition feel less like supplementation and more like daily cellular activation: a simple ritual that helps the body do more with what it has.
intelligence.mission: To reframe hydration as functional nutrition through a caffeine-free, no-added-sugar blue spirulina drink that supports everyday energy, skin, recovery, immunity, inflammation, and cellular hydration.
intelligence.vision: To own the emerging Indian space between wellness beverages, supplements, beauty-from-within, and sports hydration, with cellular health as the master narrative.
intelligence.story: FYN Wellness appears to have been born from founder-led frustration with unresolved personal health issues and the search for a more holistic product that could support the body at a cellular level. Public LinkedIn signals associate Shrey Doshi with the brand’s creation, with Hamza Patel and Avni Singhal also connected to the company’s early team.
intelligence.positioning: A cellular nutrition brand for modern Indians who want hydration, energy, skin support, and recovery in one clean daily blue ritual.
intelligence.audience: Urban, label-reading Indian consumers who are wellness-aware, premium D2C shoppers, young professionals, active lifestyle consumers, beauty-from-within buyers, and health-curious people tired of managing disconnected supplements.
intelligence.persona: Aanya is 29, lives in Bengaluru, Mumbai, or Delhi NCR, and works in strategy, product, design, consulting, marketing, or startup operations. She does Pilates or strength training, has tried protein powders, magnesium, collagen, matcha, electrolyte sachets, and skincare actives, but still feels under-recovered: dull skin, bloating, afternoon crashes, shallow sleep, and inconsistent workouts. She is drawn to FYN because cellular hydration feels like the missing layer between water, supplements, and skincare, but she needs disciplined proof because the benefit stack can sound too broad.

palette.role: Signature electric blue spirulina drink colour
Clean white packaging and negative space
Light grey ecommerce surface and UI neutral

typography.display: Modern geometric or grotesk sans-serif with bold, clean, declarative headlines.
typography.text: Simple high-legibility sans-serif suited to ecommerce, ingredient education, and benefit explanation.
typography.note: Clinical-premium rather than ornate; uppercase ingredient stacks and short proof-led paragraphs support the brand’s science-led positioning.

intelligence.logoSystem: The logo system appears minimal, with FYN Wellness as a wordmark and FYN carrying most of the memory structure. The phrase Fuel Your Nutrition. Feel the Difference. gives the abbreviation a mantra-like meaning, while the blue drink, white sachet, and cellular language are currently more distinctive than the wordmark alone.
intelligence.photographyStyle: Shoot on clean white or pale neutral surfaces with bright, cool, high-clarity lighting. Use sparse compositions: one sachet, one clear glass, vivid electric-blue liquid, powder bloom, water, or a hand performing the ritual. Keep props minimal, avoid rustic Ayurveda or dark gym aesthetics, and use centered graphic crops that make the saturated blue drink the focal point.
intelligence.packagingStyle: Premium functional nutrition in sachet-led ecommerce packaging, using white space, blue accents, product science, and convenience cues. Hydra is presented through sachet pack shots, daily ritual sachets, a vibrant blue prepared drink, and nutrition panels highlighting blue spirulina, bioactive peptides, and coconut electrolytes.
intelligence.visualIdentity: A clean clinical-premium system built around electric spirulina blue, white negative space, dark sans-serif type, and a precise functional nutrition vocabulary. The blue is not just a palette choice but the brand’s core memory asset: natural active ingredient, visual ritual, and category signal in one.

competitors.name: Fast&Up
competitors.note: A major Indian active nutrition and sports supplement brand competing on electrolytes, hydration, performance, availability, and trust among fitness consumers.

competitors.name: Wellbeing Nutrition
competitors.note: A premium Indian D2C nutrition brand spanning melts, collagen, multivitamins, beauty, sleep, gut health, and daily wellness formats; competes for the same label-reading urban wellness buyer.

competitors.name: The Whole Truth
competitors.note: Not a direct blue spirulina competitor, but a strong clean-label nutrition brand competing for consumer trust around transparent ingredients and health-led food.

competitors.name: What's Up Wellness
competitors.note: Competes in easy daily wellness rituals through supplement and gummy formats, appealing to consumers who want convenient health habits.

competitors.name: Liquid I.V.
competitors.note: A global hydration sachet reference point that competes conceptually in the electrolyte and functional hydration space, though FYN’s Indian cellular-health positioning is more specific.

social.note: Officially linked from the brand site; likely focused on product education, blue spirulina rituals, cellular hydration explainers, benefit stacks, and premium D2C launch storytelling. Cadence not verifiable from available research.

Founder-led and category-building, with educational posts around cellular health, label-reading, modern wellness behaviour, and startup ecosystem visibility. Voice is thoughtful, introspective, scientific, and more B2B or ecosystem-facing than consumer lifestyle.

press.title: BeyondSeed unveils India’s next D2C champions at the BEYONDXCELERATE pitch day at the National Stock Exchange

insights: Cellular hydration is the brand’s strongest territory and should be protected from dilution into generic wellness language.
The claim stack is broad; lead with cellular hydration and support it with two or three clearer pillars such as clean energy, recovery, and skin or gut glow.
The electric blue drink is the memory structure, not just a colour; codify blue powder bloom, blue glass rituals, sachet tears, and blue-on-white compositions.
Founder-led science should be translated into simpler consumer education: mechanism made memorable, not medical.
Hydra can bridge gym bag, skincare shelf, office drawer, and morning wellness tray without feeling confused, giving FYN a rare beauty-performance-wellness overlap.
Proof is the next creative frontier: ingredient explainers, FSSAI transparency, lab testing, expert context, customer diaries, and carefully handled 30-day ritual content will be crucial.

catalog.name: Hydra
catalog.description: Normal hydration stops at the surface. Hydra reaches your cells. Supercharged by Blue Spirulina, the only superfood with no cell wall. That means 95% of it absorbs directly, compared to 30% from most things you consume. Nothing locked out. Nothing wasted. The blue pigment inside, Phycocyanin, acts as a cellular antioxidant, protecting and repairing at the deepest level. Bioactive peptides reach your cells directly, no conversion needed. And the electrolytes come straight from

rejectedShots.prompt: Nikon Z7 II with a 50mm f/1.8 lens, 45° front-left product angle, clean modern colour grade with cool blue bias and soft film grain; the only honest camera imperfection is a slightly off-centre crop. Position the product exactly as in the reference image on frosted glass, angled for dimensionality, against a seamless product-colour-matched ice-blue background. Keep styling to one restrained stainless shaker cue placed far behind as a blurred vertical highlight and one transparent glass of vivid blue drink near the product base. Cool high-clarity light from the left produces real contact shadows, metal speculars and glass refraction. The image feels premium, clinical and sensorial without becoming sterile or rendered.

Canon EOS R5 with a 35mm f/2 lens, true 90° overhead flat-lay, Kodak Portra 400-style cool whites and gentle roll-off; the only honest camera imperfection is fine film grain. Arrange the product exactly as in the reference image flat or standing as physically plausible from the reference, on white acrylic with a seamless product-colour-matched soft ice-blue field filling the frame. Compose with disciplined negative space, a clear glass of shockingly blue hydration drink, two ice cubes, and a small restrained warm coconut-neutral cue in #F4EFE8, kept secondary. Bright cool directional light creates crisp object shadows that prove real height and contact. Product label, logo and callouts remain sharp, correctly oriented and legible.
negatives: no invented product orientation that contradicts reference

rejectedShots.prompt: Hasselblad X2D 100C with a 55mm f/2.5 lens, strict top-down 90° composition, restrained cool film grain and soft highlight roll-off; the only honest camera imperfection is a faint blown highlight on one ice cube. Set the product exactly as in the reference image on glossy white acrylic, surrounded by clean product-colour-matched FYN blue negative space. Create a minimal cellular hydration scene with a transparent glass bottle of shockingly blue drink aligned diagonally, condensation beads, and one small stainless shaker cue cropped at the edge. Bright cool light produces crisp shadows and believable reflections on acrylic. The frame is editorial, controlled, product-only and unmistakably FYN.

Sony A7 IV with a 40mm f/2.5 lens, photographed directly overhead at 90°, Fujifilm Pro 400H cool clinical grade; the only honest camera imperfection is one hard-edged shadow from the product falling consistently rightward. Place the product exactly as in the reference image on pale stone within a seamless product-colour-matched FYN blue set. Build a minimal hydration ritual flat-lay: a transparent bottle with vivid blue liquid, ice, a few water droplets on the stone, and no other clutter. The composition is graphic but real, with the product leading the frame and the blue liquid acting as proof of the drink mix. Cool high-clarity light creates believable contact shadows and surface texture.
negatives: no misspelled or duplicated label text
no utensils unless restrained and physically plausible
no random cubes pebbles or confetti
